axiom ImprovedCredit : obj → Prop
axiom AffordDownPayment : obj → Prop
axiom QualifyMortgage : obj → Prop
axiom BuyDreamHome : obj → Prop

axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, SavedAggressively x → AffordDownPayment x
-- If they improved their credit score, they could qualify for a mortgage.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ImprovedCredit x → QualifyMortgage x
-- If someone qualifies for a mortgage, they can buy their dream home.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, QualifyMortgage x → BuyDreamHome x
-- Someone either saves aggressively or improves their credit.
axiom R4 : SavedAggressively someone ∨ ImprovedCredit someone
-- Given that they could not afford a down payment.
axiom Hnot : ¬AffordDownPayment someone

-- Cannot prove ¬BuyDreamHome someone from given axioms; in fact, we can derive BuyDreamHome someone:
theorem can_buy : BuyDreamHome someone := by
  -- From ¬AffordDownPayment and (Saved → AffordDownPayment), deduce ¬Saved
  have h_not_saved : ¬SavedAggressively someone := by
    intro hs
    exact Hnot (R1 someone hs)
  -- From Saved ∨ Improved and ¬Saved, deduce Improved
  have h_improved : ImprovedCredit someone := by
    cases R4 with
    | inl hs =>
        exact False.elim (Hnot (R1 someone hs))
    | inr hi =>
        exact hi
  -- Then qualify and buy
  have h_qual : QualifyMortgage someone := R2 someone h_improved
  exact R3 someone h_qual
</lean>

axiom obj : Type
axiom Lakers : obj

axiom StrongPerimeterDefense : obj → Prop
axiom ProtectPaint : obj → Prop
axiom ForceManyTurnovers : obj → Prop
axiom LimitInsideScoring : obj → Prop

-- If a team plays strong perimeter defense, then they will force many turnovers.
axiom R1 : ∀ t : obj, StrongPerimeterDefense t → ForceManyTurnovers t
-- If the big men protect the paint, then they will limit inside scoring.
axiom R2 : ∀ t : obj, ProtectPaint t → LimitInsideScoring t
-- Either the perimeter defense will be strong or the paint will be protected (maybe both).
axiom R3 : ∀ t : obj, StrongPerimeterDefense t ∨ ProtectPaint t

-- From the premises, the conditional in question is actually provable:
theorem lakers_turnovers_if_not_limit :
  (¬LimitInsideScoring Lakers) → ForceManyTurnovers Lakers := by
  intro hNotLIS
  -- From ¬(LimitInsideScoring), infer ¬(ProtectPaint) via contrapositive of R2
  have hNotPP : ¬ProtectPaint Lakers := by
    intro hPP
    have hLIS : LimitInsideScoring Lakers := R2 Lakers hPP
    exact hNotLIS hLIS
  -- From the disjunction and ¬(ProtectPaint), deduce StrongPerimeterDefense
  have hSPD : StrongPerimeterDefense Lakers := by
    have hOr := R3 Lakers
    cases hOr with
    | inl hspd => exact hspd
    | inr hpp =>
        have : False := hNotPP hpp
        exact False.elim this
  -- Then conclude turnovers from strong perimeter defense
  exact R1 Lakers hSPD

-- Since we can prove (¬LimitInsideScoring Lakers) → ForceManyTurnovers Lakers,
-- we cannot prove its negation from the given premises.
</lean>

    intro hB

axiom R1v : ∀ x : Person, SpeaksDaily x → Vocabulary x

axiom R4 : ∀ x : Person, ListenPodcasts x → ListeningComp x

  intro hNotRC
  have hdisj : SpeaksDaily Emma ∨ ListenPodcasts Emma := R3 Emma
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hSpeak =>
      have hFl : Fluency Emma := R1f Emma hSpeak
      have hRC : RealConversations Emma := R2 Emma hFl
      exact False.elim (hNotRC hRC)
  | inr hPod =>
      exact R4 Emma hPod

-- Therefore, we cannot prove the negation of the statement from the given premises.

  intro hns

  have h_not_exists_add : ¬(∃ x : Person, AddsSalt x) := by
    intro hex
    cases hex with
    | intro x hx =>
      have hs : Savory := R1 x hx
      exact hns hs
  -- Thus the stew is not Thick
  have h_not_thick : ¬Thick := Or.elim Law1 (fun hex => False.elim (h_not_exists_add hex)) (fun h => h)
  -- Tasting implies Thick
  have tastes_implies_thick : ∀ x : Person, Tastes x → Thick :=
    fun x hx => R2 x (R3 x hx)
  -- Therefore, no one tastes the stew

    intro hex
    cases hex with
    | intro x hx =>
      have ht : Thick := tastes_implies_thick x hx
      exact h_not_thick ht
  -- From the secret disjunction, conclude someone prepares a salad
  exact Or.elim Secret (fun hex => False.elim (not_exists_taste hex)) (fun h => h)

-- They also needed to go grocery shopping (treat as: they go shopping).

  have md : MakeDinner Sam := R2 Sam TGo
  have cl : Close Sam :=
    Or.elim (R3 Sam) (fun c => c) (fun notMD => False.elim (notMD md))
  exact R1 Sam cl

-- Cannot prove ¬(StartCooking Sam → PantryEmpty Sam) from given axioms.
</lean>

axiom NaturalAbility : obj → Prop
axiom TopYouth : obj → Prop

-- If someone was a top youth player, then they have natural ability.

  intro hnpw
  intro hp

theorem top_implies_talented (x : obj) : TopYouth x → Talented x := by
  intro hty
  have hna : NaturalAbility x := R5 x hty
  have hps : PickedStart x := R4 x hna
  exact R2 x hps

theorem not_top_of_not_talented (x : obj) : ¬Talented x → ¬TopYouth x := by
  intro hnotT
  intro hTop
  exact hnotT (top_implies_talented x hTop)

-- Hence we cannot prove the negation of the statement from the premises.
</lean>
